observations.recommendation:
To address the deficiencies and mitigate the associated risks, we recommended the
following and Management agreed to:

a. Develop and Implement Written Policies and Procedures:
• Based on the customized procedures, draft a Cash Management Manual for
MGB. Use the NGAS Manual procedures as a framework but adapt them
to the MGB’s specific needs and challenges. This manual should include:
 o Roles and Responsibilities: Clearly define the duties of collectors, tellers,
 liquidating officers, the treasurer, and the accountant.
 o Step-by-Step Procedures: Provide detailed instructions for collections,
 remittances, deposits, and reconciliations.
 o Forms and Templates: Include templates for RCDs, JEVs, and other
 required documents.
 o Timelines: Specify deadlines for remittances, deposits, and
 reconciliations.
 o Sanctions for Non-Compliance: Outline penalties for violations of the
 policies (e.g., delayed remittances, unsupported disbursements).

b. Monitor and Evaluate Compliance
• Establish a monitoring system to track compliance with the policies. For
example:
 o Conduct monthly examination of cash collections and deposits.
 o Perform surprise cash counts to verify the accuracy of records.
 o Review RCDs and JEVs regularly to ensure timely and accurate
 reporting.
 o Evaluate the effectiveness of the policies annually and update them as
 needed.
• Ensure that reconciliations are conducted at least quarterly, or more
frequently if required by management.

c. Address Operational Challenges:
• Provide secure transport mechanisms for field collectors to facilitate timely
remittances.
• Implement measures to reduce delays caused by travel time and distance.

d. Impose Sanctions for Non-Compliance:
• Establish a system of sanctions for violations of cash management policies
and procedures with a graduated system (e.g., warnings, suspensions) to
ensure adherence.

e. Conduct Regular Training and Dissemination:
• Conduct training sessions for all employees involved in cash management to
ensure they understand the new policies and procedures.
• Distribute the Cash Management Manual to all relevant staff and make it
accessible for reference.

To address the identified gaps and ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 97-
002 and COA Circular 2018-003 (ICSPPS), management is advised and agreed to
implement the following recommendations, as far as practicable:

a. Strengthen monitoring and enforcement
• The accounting unit will implement a real-time tracking system to monitor
cash advance aging and flag delays exceeding 30 days. Weekly reports
should be generated for the Municipal Mayor and department heads.
• The accounting unit will notify accountable officers five days before
deadlines and issue formal demand letters for delays beyond 30 days, with
copies sent to the Municipal Mayor.

b. Enforce accountability measures
• Suspend salary releases for officers with overdue advances until full
liquidation or refund is completed.
• Apply penalties (e.g., reprimands, suspensions) for repeat offenders and
include timely liquidation compliance in performance evaluations.
• Require officers to promptly return cash advances following a trip
cancellation, enforce penalties for any delays, and accurately document the
cancellation dates.

c. Conduct training and capacity building
• Hold quarterly training sessions on COA Circular No. 97-002, using case
studies to underscore non-compliance consequences.

d. Institute preventive controls
• The accounting unit will conduct a mid-year review to ensure all cash
advances comply with year-end liquidation rules.

To mitigate these challenges and improve SEF management, we recommended and
the Management agreed to take the following integrated measures:

a. Strengthen Internal Controls and Tracking Systems:
• Develop and implement a robust tracking system to ensure that the
approved SEF Budget is transmitted to the Municipal Budget Officer,
Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer within the mandated
seven-day period.
• Maintain clear documentation of receipt and transmission dates, which will
serve as the foundation for timely fund disbursement and effective financial
oversight.
• Designate specific personnel for the timely preparation and submission of
quarterly and annual utilization reports, ensuring alignment with
regulatory deadlines.

b. Improve Oversight and Accountability:
• Introduce regular internal audits and monitoring practices to promptly
identify and address procedural deviations.
• Provide ongoing training on SEF guidelines to ensure that all staff
understand and effectively implement regulatory requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed, with the Municipal Treasury Office (MTO) and the Municipal Accounting Office (MACCO) as leads, agreed to strengthen its cash management by further enhancing its processes relating to the reconciliation, recording and presentation of its Cash and Cash Accounts through:
a. streamlining the reconciliation process by (a.) coordinating with the depository banks and securing copies of and information as regards existing debit and credit advices, and (b.) adequately reflecting the adjusted/reconciled balances on both the MTO's cashbooks and that of the accounting's records; and
b. revisiting the financial/treasury records processing and input systems/programs, and considering the possibility of having a single and dynamic computer software and program interconnected between that of the concerned offices such that financial transactions are automatically posted and/or inputted and could be easily tracked/traced.

We recommended that the LGUs of the province further enhance the
quality of its individual LDRRM management processes by taking
concrete actions to address the issues related to manpower, data and
information, and formalization to reinforce the establishment of an
enabling level of coordination in a collective setting and ensure disaster
resiliency.

constant follow-ups with the input providers-suppliers for the

beforehand.

concrete actions to address issues related to manpower and
formalization of the office to ensure effective and efficient
management of all PPE owned by the LGU;

The Actual date of remittance of collection and deposit as well as check issuance and liquidation were reflected in the books and in the Reference column of the Cash Book. Series of Accountable Forms/Official Receipts or Reference Reports of Collections and Deposits number were indicated in the reference column of the Cash Book. Reconciliation is being done regularly and the Cash Book was updated based on Reports of Collections and Deposits.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore advised the Management to:

a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant to strictly comply with the requirement of Republic Act No. 10121,
guided by the accounting and reporting procedures under COA Circular No. 2012-
002; and
b. After transferring to the trust liability account, the Municipal Treasurer should
immediately deposit the unexpended balance to the LDRRMF bank account.

As remedial measures to the foregoing, we recommended to:

a. Conduct a complete physical count of all assets owned by the Municipality;
b. Address all the causes of the deficiencies/gaps noted to enable concerned
officials to comply with existing rules and regulations on the submission of
required property inventory report, keeping and updating of required property
and accounting records, and reconciliation of same in order to arrive at the
correct balances of the PPEs;
c. Ensure that payments for every purchased property/equipment shall be made
only when there is evidence that same has been duly recorded in the PPELC the
acquisitions, additions and dispositions of the PPEs by the Property Custodian and/or
Municipal Accountant;
d. Exert more efforts to secure the documents of properties not provided with
depreciation, and implement Paragraphs 43 and 59 of PPSAS which requires such
provision for depreciation; and
e. Identify unserviceable properties and record the same in the Inventory and
Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to facilitate the disposal and
eventual dropping from the books.

We recommended that the LGUs of the province collectively and individually
ensure the effective and efficient delivery of LDRRM responsibilities by enhancing the level
of coordination through managing the challenges on the dependencies that affect the
essential management processes.

We recommended that the LGUs of the province ensure the effective and efficient
execution of disaster responses through the establishment of appropriately contextualized
disaster protocols.

observations.managementAction: Update the cashbook based on the Report of Collections and Deposits submitted by the Revenue Liquidating Officer on a daily basis; Reflect the actual date of remittance of collection and actual date of deposit of the collection in the cashbooks of the Municipal Treasurer; Reflect the series of accountable forms or the RCD number in the Reference column of the cashbook. At least once per quarter, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of their books and to reflect all reconciling items in both books.
observations.reason: There was recently efforts and new arrangements or system are being implemented/ tested to improve the updating of the books every 5th of the month and to properly furnish COA with the made.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the cash accountable officer should maintain Reports of Collections and Deposits properly by organizing and filing them completely to facilitate easy collections and retrieval of records for validation and/or verification purposes. Further, we recommended that each revenue collection clerk should maintain a cashbook that will serve as permanent record of transactions for future references.
observations.managementAction: In lieu of Cash Book, liquidation reports are being prepared to ascertain the completeness of collection of the accountable officer.
observations.reason: In lieu of Cash Book, liquidation reports are being prepared to ascertain the completeness of collection of the accountable officer.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following:  Review the amounts presented in the Financial Statements and consider the manner of inventory purchases of the Municipality since all supplies once purchased were already distributed to the requesting department/office;  Provide bodega for the delivered inventories for storage prior to distribution/withdrawal to employ the Perpetual Inventory Method, if there are no budgetary constraints. Once provided, maintain the SLC and the stock cards upon delivery of purchased inventories and use the Moving Average Method for costing inventories in conformity with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The GSO to comply with the audit recommendations. Inventory Reports were submitted to COA dated June 7, 2018.
observations.reason: As per EO No.11 dated August 5, 2016 inventory had been conducted as last year’s. PPAs were incorporated in the MDRRMF. FLUP and allocation were submitted.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, in view of the above observations/gaps noted, we reiterated our recommendation of 52% of the assets recorded in the books of accounts to: - Conduct a comprehensive physical count of PPE headed by GSO to identify the properties under the Trust Fund and transfer the same to the General Fund and subject to depreciation. - Retrieve all necessary documents in MPDO and MEO for the conduct of inventory. - Issue Inventory Custodian Slip on properties considered as small tangible items pursuant to COA Circular 2005-02 (Annex C) in coordination with COA Circular No. 97-005 (Annex D); - Maintain and update the PPELC as well as the different property ledgers; - Maintain and update property cards by the Property Custodian; - Reconcile the PPELC, property cards and the physical counted properties - Exert all efforts to secure the documents of properties not provided with depreciation; and/or - Apprise all properties not provided with depreciation and make a sound decision on the amounts of impairments to presents fairly the balances of the PPE and then provide depreciation; - On trust fund properties - Exert effort to locate documents related to the booked-up properties under Trust Fund; - Secure documents related to the PPEs recorded in the Trust Fund books, if none can be retrieved from the file of the Municipality, by communicating with the funding agency; and - Transfer all completed projects’ properties to General Fund and subject the same to depreciation.
observations.managementAction: On-going efforts were exerted for the conduct of inventory. Reports were submitted to COA dated June 7, 2018. Retrieve documents and transfer completed projects properties to General Fund.
observations.reason: Succeeding actions were taken as per EO No.11 dated August 5, 2016. Inventory Reports were submitted to COA dated June 7, 2018.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following:  Require the submission of the completed vouchers of expenses that needs to be accrued at the end of the year from different offices concerned to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for proper recording of the payable; and  Record the accrual of expenses in the books of the accountant in the period of its occurrence following the accrual accounting principle
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were made to long outstanding accounts and vouchers were recorded in the following period.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, the audit team recommended the following:  Record the charges amounting to P1,219,086.45 in the books of the Municipality as a receivable account and credit the same to the appropriate income account;  Require the person liable to settle the charges before the issuance of the Certificate of Examination (COE);  Require the Municipal Treasurer to make an analysis on the payments made by the Accountable Officer relative to the NC/NFD and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording.  Submit to the audit team the status of the settlement of NC/NFD in a quarterly basis.
observations.managementAction: Record the charges in the books of the Municipality and require the person liable to settle the charges before the issuance of the COE.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, the audit team recommended that management, in its future undertakings relative to CIF, must adhere to item no. 6.1.5 of the COA-DBM-DILG-GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 8, 2015 by limiting the cash advance of the SDO up to the 3-month requirement of the Municipality as regards to the CIF Physical and Financial Plan to ensure safekeeping of government funds. Moreover, we required the submission of a written justification on the cash advance made.
observations.managementAction: Required the SDO to limit the cash advance up to the 3-month requirement as per Joint Circular and submit justification on the cash advance.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, the team recommended the following:  Stop paying services that are not actually rendered otherwise the same will be subjected to suspension/disallowance;  Accompany claims with duly approved daily time records and other necessary documents supporting the claim (e.g. approved authority, lesson plan, etc.)
observations.managementAction: Stopped payments for services not actually rendered and required accompaniment of claims with duly approved daily time records and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following: a. Strictly adhere to the provision of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 specifically on posting of Request for Quotation for procurements amounting to above P50,000.00 for a period of three (3) calendar days in the PhilGEPS website, website of the municipality, if available, and at any conspicuous place reserved for the purpose in the premises of the municipality. b. Payments for meals and snacks should be accompanied by list of participants and program design to ensure the priority and legality of the transaction. As such, the Municipal Accountant is enjoined to submit the lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Request for quotations are now properly posted in the Philgeps as per 2016 Revised IRR of 9184. Lacking documents are now properly submitted.
observations.reason: Request for quotations are now properly posted in the Philgeps as per 2016 Revised IRR of 9184.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following: a. Initiative a move to follow-up the approval of the allocated programs stated therein; b. Allocate an amount for the implementation of program/projects to address present environmental concerns of the municipality. c. Implement the planned program/projects to address environmental concerns of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: SWM 10 years plan was submitted last December 2016. PPAs were incorporated in the MDRRMF. FLUP and allocation were submitted.
observations.reason: SWM 10 years plan was submitted last December 2016. PPAs were incorporated in the MDRRMF. FLUP and allocation were submitted.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain cashbooks for each project whether or not a separate bank account is maintained pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office maintain separate sets of ledger for each of the enterprise/special accounts and prepare separate trial balances and financial statements reflecting their respective financial condition and results of operations.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Accounting Office prepare separate sets of ledger for each of the enterprise/special accounts and prepare separate trial balances and financial statements reflecting the respective financial condition and results of their operations, in accordance with existing regulations. Succeeding month to the Accounting Office for immediate recording thus submission of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office maintain sub-set of financial accounts consisting of separate sets of ledgers for each of the enterprise/special accounts and consequently prepare separate trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of their respective operations, in accordance with existing regulations.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that management enjoin the Municipal accountant to strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the submission of paid disbursement vouchers and supporting documents and other financial reports. DVs and its supporting documents as well as report of collection and deposit must be submitted every 5th day of the same to COA on the 10th day of the ensuing month.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum was issued to strictly implement the closing of the books every 5th of the month and to properly furnish COA with the made.
observations.reason: Please be reminded that non-submission of these documents is sanctioned by Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code. Efforts and new arrangements or system are being implemented/ tested to improve the updating of the books every 5th of the month and to properly furnish COA with the made.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following: a. Require all barangays to submit complete documents, feasibility reports, before the proposal of the annual budget for 20% DF to ensure implementation of that portion of the DF funds related to barangay requested projects; b. Ensure that budget proposals for DF projects considers price escalations and other factors to ensure that enough allocated budget is provided for each proposed project; c. Submit Annual Budget to Sangguniang Bayan on time if not earlier and make representation for the prompt enactment of the Annual Budget. d. Require focal persons to fast track the implementation of the unimplemented PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Require all barangays to submit complete documents and feasibility reports; ensure that budget proposals consider price escalations; submit Annual Budget on time and require focal persons to fast track implementation of the unimplemented PPAs.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that management should follow the order of priority in deducting the mandatory and allowable deductions to the salaries of the employees.
observations.reason: Loans of personnel requires salary deduction (as embodied in the MOA), thus management could not limit the deduction as to what is only required by law for humanitarian reasons.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that management enjoin the Municipal accountant and other departments submitting reports to the Accounting Office to strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the submission of paid disbursement vouchers including supporting documents, disbursement vouchers and other Financial Reports.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of documents from the different concerned Offices of the Municipality to the Office of the Accountant hence reports were prepared late. Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following which the management committed to follow: Exert effort to secure the necessary documents and reconstruct the other receivable account’s subsidiary ledger to support the balances of the account as presented in the Financial Statements; Confirm the amount of the reconstructed receivables to identify which of the receivables are still valid receivable of the Municipality. Make necessary adjustments in the books of accounts if proven to be without existing debtor for an accurate presentation of the other receivable account in the Financial Statements; supported with complete documents.
observations.reason: Lack of documents to reconstruct the receivable accounts.

Therefore, we recommended that collectors will accomplish the RCD before the cash will be turned-over to the Liquidating Officer and the Liquidating Officer will do the same prior to the remittance of the cash to the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all collections pertaining to a particular fund will be deposited to the correct bank account.
observations.reason: Most of the Collectors lack technical know-how in manipulating the computer thus reports were prepared in bulk by single person.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following: Request for a permanent personnel (preferably with accounting related background) to focus on the preparation of the bank reconciliation report; Keep bank reconciliation report updated on a monthly basis especially as of year-end for every depository account of the Municipality; Enroll Municipality’s bank accounts to the “We Access” program of the depository bank to be updated on the balance of the account and preparation of an updated bank reconciliation was possible; Exert effort to trace the documents for the unrecorded bank accounts and record the same in the Cash in Bank balance; Request for an SB resolution to close the two accounts and transfer the same to the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: All of the bank accounts were with bank reconcilia report except for MEEDO and time deposit accounts.
observations.reason: The account was overlooked due to failure of Municipal Treasurer to submit the passbook for the time deposit and the bank statement from depository bank. Reiterated in AAR 2016.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended closing the petty cash funds at the end of the year by requiring the petty cash custodian to liquidate all expenses incurred and remitting the remaining balance to the Municipal Treasurer, if any, confirmed by an official receipt, in consonance with the COA circular.
observations.managementAction: Amount was already liquidated and adjusted in the General Fund Books

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we reiterated our recommendation since no action had been undertaken by the Management to change the scheme of the remittance of the collection of collectors to the Liquidating Officer. The remittance/liquidation should be in accordance with Section 29 of NGAS Volume 1, that is, to remit to the Liquidating Officer all collections made during the day at the close of each business day for those situated near the Municipality’s Office and at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach P5,000.00 for those Collectors whose official station will take more than one day to travel. Further, we recommended that no additional accountable forms will be issued to the Collectors unless previously issued forms were already remitted/liquidated to the liquidating officer.
observations.managementAction: Collectors were required to remit on time.
observations.reason: Some of the Collectors were assigned on places far from the Municipal Office.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following measures for the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to present fairly the Cash in Bank account in the Financial Statements: Coordinate and reconcile the book errors/reconciling items; Submit all disbursement vouchers, especially those documents with checks already issued, to the Accounting Office for recording; Conduct monthly reconciliation of the Municipal Accountant’s book and the Municipal Treasurer’s Cashbook to trace immediately reconciling items and adjustments will be recorded abruptly.
observations.managementAction: Current reconciling items were adjusted however long outstanding reconciling items were not settled.
observations.reason: Lack of necessary supporting documents to make the adjusting entries. Reiterated in AAR 2016.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following: Trace the cause of the discrepancy and make the necessary adjustments in the books of either the Treasurer’s or the Accountant’s, whichever had errors in recording; Reconcile the books/records of the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant on a regular basis; and All discrepancies noted must be acted immediately.

Therefore, we reiterated our recommendation as last year that management do the following: Exert effort to demand immediately the liquidation of all outstanding cash advances from the concerned officials/employees who are still connected to the Municipality and consider taking legal actions/remedy against them. Impose sanctions prescribed in the said Circulars for non-compliance thereof, such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand, including filing appropriate charges against them; Gather all documents that will prove that liquidation of the named individuals is no longer possible such as those who are already deceased (e.g. death certificate) and whose last address is not known (e.g. retirement certification, clearances, etc.) and request for the write off of the receivable for fair presentation of the account in the Financial Statements taking into consideration the liability of those individuals who granted the clearances.
observations.reason: Most of the accounts were under litigation and some could no longer be located. Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following to support the assertion of the Municipality on its advances/receivables from Officers and Employees of the Municipality: Assign/Hire an accounting graduate personnel to focus on the maintenance/update of the ageing schedules; Update/Maintain the ageing schedule of the Advances for Officers and Employees and Due from Officers and Employees account; Exert effort to secure the necessary documents and provide the ageing schedules for Trust Fund advances.
observations.managementAction: Supporting schedule only indicates a total of P301,572.03 while FS balance as of December 31, 2016 amounted to P324,693.29 thus a discrepancy of P23,121.26
observations.reason: Lack of supporting documents

observations.managementAction: Inventory of unservicea ble properties were already made Management is in the process of locating the documents and reconcile the records. Physical count was already done. Report is under review by top level management.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel / manpower to do the activity and the lack of records to support the amounts shown in the financial statements. Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that Management analyze the long-outstanding dormant accounts and, if these are found to be no longer valid claims, necessary reversing/correcting entries should be taken-up in the books pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445. Moreover, the negative balances have to be analyzed and adjusted accordingly. Furthermore, we recommended to stop the practice of transferring the amounts due to the different entities to another trust fund account instead it should be deducted directly from the source fund and booked as a payable account under the fund it referred to.
observations.managementAction: Substantia l accounts were either reclassed/ remitted/ reversed/ adjusted.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the management to submit the lacking documents such as Daily Time Records, Approved Application for Leave and Locator Slips. It is also recommended that all concerned employees must comply with PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 specifically on accomplishing the above supporting documents for Salaries and Wages.
observations.managementAction: All claims for salaries and wages were with daily time records.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the management should adhere to the principle of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in the disbursement of government funds by attaching all necessary documents to support the above claims/expenses, otherwise, these shall be suspended or disallowed in audit. Henceforth, the management should ensure complete documentation of every transaction and adhere to the rules and regulations as provided above before payment is made to the payee in consonance with pertinent rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management already attached the necessary supporting documents to expenses charged against the 20% development fund.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the GAD Focal Point System should attend more seminars and trainings on the process of GAD planning and budgeting specifically on the use of HGDG tool and collection of sex disaggregated data to ensure that PPAs to be identified are gender responsive and clearly address a gender issue.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Budget were already submitted to the DILG.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to focus on the workload.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following to ensure that Cash in Bank balance is fairly presented in the Financial Statements: Exert effort to trace the documents for the unrecorded bank accounts and include the same in the Cash in Bank balance; Refer to the granting agency if no liquidation report is necessary to close the grant; If remaining amount will accrue to the benefit of the Municipality, revert balances of the account to the General Fund to close the existing ARC-ADB and STARCM accounts.
observations.managementAction: Bank account in questioned was already recorded in the books

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be dedicated in maintaining and submitting the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and Report on obsolete, spoiled and cancelled accountable forms to trace the accuracy and completeness of the reported collection of the Collectors as well as to protect the Government for possible misappropriation of government funds.
observations.managementAction: A new MTO personnel was assigned to look into to prompt preparation and submission of the report.
observations.reason: Lack of documents to support the liquidation of the accountable forms. Reiterated in AAR 2016.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following: At least once per quarter, pursuant to the above ruling, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of their books and to reflect all reconciling items in both books affected to support the assertion of the Municipality on the balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA, Cash in Vault and Payroll Fund accounts as presented in the Financial Statement; Reflect the actual date of remittance of collection and actual date of deposit of the collection in the cashbooks of the Municipal Treasurer; Reflect the actual date of checks issued for cash advances and the actual date of liquidation of the cash advances in the cashbook of the Municipal Treasurer; Indicate the series of accountable forms or the RCD number in the Reference column of the cashbook.
observations.managementAction: Reconcilia tion of accounts were started however adjustments were not promptly recorded.
observations.reason: Delayed or unposted reconciling items on the books of Municipal treasurer hence adjustments could not be cleared.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following: Validate and account the details of the unidentified CIP amounting to P26,042,261.61 by coordinating closely with other concerned department heads particularly with the Municipal Engineer; Make necessary adjustments to the books on identified completed projects with proper supporting documents; Maintain separate CIPLC on per project basis; and Ensure that only on-going and uncompleted projects are reflected in CIP.
observations.managementAction: Located some of the records and transferre d the same to appropriate accounts.
observations.reason: Lack of supporting documents from previous accountants of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the following to the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian (Municipal Treasurer) to ensure correct balances of the inventory account in the financial statements: Employ the Perpetual Inventory Method; Maintain the SLC and the stock cards; Use the Moving Average Method for costing inventories in conformity with existing regulations.
observations.reason: Lack of storage where all supplies/material/inventory bought could be kept to employ perpetual method of accounting for inventories. Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended that all projects funded under PDAF and DAP funds shall be discontinued and to remit the remaining amount to the Bureau of Treasury in the light of the above decision of the Supreme Court.
observations.managementAction: With the return of funds to the Bureau of Treasury, this audit observation is already moot and academic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management establish a municipal council for the elderly and PWDs, allocate at least one percent of its budget as required in the General Provisions of the GAA and prepare program/projects to enhance their welfare and safety.
observations.managementAction: Some of the activities of the Seniors and the PWDs were attributed to the different funds.
observations.reason: No Resolution to that effect.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: The management should closely monitor the utilization and implementation of priority projects for the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and to maximize the usage of the fund. The implementing department concerned should fast track the implementation based on the scheduled time frames indicated in the AIP to achieve the desired management outcomes. Strict compliance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the Department of Interior and Local Government and Department of Budget and Management dated April 13, 2011 should be observed.
observations.managementAction: Personnel in-charge started the implementation of prior year’s PAPs.
observations.reason: Delayed approval of the Annual Budget, Election Ban, long process of bidding and the weather conditions which affects the implementati on of the projects. Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Disposal Committee adhere and implement the COA Revised Guidelines on Appraisal of Property and prepare Agency Appraisal Report to facilitate proper evaluation and disposal of these unserviceable properties and waste materials the soonest possible time. Also, we recommend that all unserviceable properties and waste materials should be properly recorded and accounted for and reclassified to Other Asset account.
observations.managementAction: The lists of some identified unservicea ble properties were already provided. However, the disposal of the properties cannot be made due to additional documents required by COA prior to its inspection.
observations.reason: The disposal was not yet done due to lack of documents required by COA for the appraisal of the properties for disposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should ensure that a copy of contracts/purchase order, irrespective of amount, and all supporting documents relative thereto is submitted to the Office of the Auditor concerned within five (5) days from issuance thereof, pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-010 so timely review can be made and inform management of differences, should there be any.
observations.managementAction: Submitted Purchase Orders and all supporting documents of those projects requested for technical inspection by COA.

observations.recommendation: In view of the Supreme Court Decision declaring PDAF/DAP as unconstitutional, remit/return the funds not utilized to the Department of Finance.
observations.managementAction: Job orders were with contracts indicating therein their respective work assignments, workloads and responsibilities.
observations.reason: On the process of evaluation of their policy guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job orders in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO No. 292 and grant the rates of salaries pursuant to Section 210 Volume I of the GAAM. And under existing labor laws otherwise employees should be hired as casual or emergency staff. Further, if merited under the existing Civil Service rules and regulations, the performance of the said job order personnel should be evaluated for possible appointment to permanent plantilla positions.
observations.managementAction: Job orders were with contracts indicating therein their respective work assignments, workloads and responsibi-lities.
observations.reason: On the process of evaluation of their policy guidelines.
